title: Catalog↔config relation_type drift: task_gated_by_item consumed by a shipped derivation but missing from the packaged catalog — a class with no parity forcing-function
question: Why does a fresh accept-all substrate lack task_gated_by_item; what is the root cause, shape, and reproducible condition; is it an atomic omission or an instance of a broader class; and does the substrate already track it?
method: git archaeology on the catalog (samples/conception.json) and live .context/config.json (git log -S per relation_type), pi-context CLI substrate inspection (read-config / read-samples-catalog / context-init→accept-all→install on a throwaway /tmp substrate), source trace of the write-time edge validator, and a prior-art search of framework-gaps/decisions by topic
findings_summary: task_gated_by_item was hand-added to the live .context/config.json on 2026-06-07 (commit 669890c, to author the TASK-033→FEAT-006 edge) and never back-ported to the packaged catalog samples/conception.json; the one catalog gating-relation back-port (a582939 / TASK-061) was scoped to FGAP-090's three new relations and did not include this pre-existing sibling, so it fell between the cracks. It is NOT atomic: the catalog relation_types[] (34) is a strict subset of the live config (40), missing six live-only relations — task_gated_by_item, session_touches_item, task_advances_story, feature_advances_story, decision_derived_from_item, decision_escalates_underdetermined — each hand-added to live config (for an edge/lens/invariant) and never back-ported, with no forcing-function ensuring the catalog carries the vocabulary the shipped derivations consume and no record distinguishing intentionally-substrate-local from catalog-worthy. SHAPE: on a fresh install-ceremony substrate task_gated_by_item is not merely inert to gate-aware currentState (FGAP-061) but UNWRITABLE — the registration-first write-time validator validateEdgeAgainstRegistry (context-sdk.ts:1496-1502) short-circuits on the unregistered relation_type and assertEdgeValidForWrite (:1548) throws, so a task gate cannot be expressed at all while feature/story/decision gating ships. PRIOR-ART: the substrate did not track this class — nearest neighbors FGAP-060 (catalog→substrate propagation, opposite direction) and FGAP-010 (a build-time parity forcing-function over the code-surface trio, the structural precedent). DISPOSITION: filed as FGAP-094 (class-level, P2) and decomposed into TASK-066 (back-port the consumed-but-missing relations) + TASK-067 (a build-time catalog⊇consumed-vocabulary parity gate mirroring scripts/parity-check.ts). The catalog-worthiness rule is derivable — a relation_type is catalog-required iff a shipped src derivation consumes it by literal string — so the five non-consumed live-only relations are correctly substrate-local. The decomposition surfaced an adjacent instance of the same class beyond this document's six: phase_depends_on, consumed by shipped roadmap-plan.ts (:329/:505/:528) yet registered in NEITHER the catalog nor live config, folded into TASK-066/067.
stale_conditions: FGAP-094 is resolved / the catalog reaches parity with the consumed vocabulary (the drift finding becomes historical)
TASK-066 lands (task_gated_by_item + phase_depends_on back-ported) — the unwritable-on-fresh-substrate symptom is gone
TASK-067 lands (the build-time catalog⊇consumed-vocabulary parity gate) — the no-forcing-function class finding is closed
the catalog relation_types[] or live config relation_types[] change materially from the 2026-06-16 baseline
the write-time edge validator's registration-first short-circuit (context-sdk.ts) changes

citations.label: the full investigation (root-cause git evidence, shape, repro, prior-art table, 6-member live-only diff)
live config relation_types[] — task_gated_by_item present (669890c), strict superset
registration-first write-time edge validator (unwritable on fresh substrate)
FGAP-094 — the class-level gap this audit grounds
